title: Mendelian-randomization analysis finds no causal effect of microbiota immaturity on child growth
abstract: Using host genetic variants that shape gut community maturation as instruments, we performed a Mendelian-randomization study across three cohorts to test whether microbiota immaturity causally contributes to undernutrition. Genetically predicted immaturity showed no significant effect on weight-for-age or stunting, while reverse-direction tests supported growth status driving community state. The genetic evidence argues against a causal contribution of microbiota immaturity to childhood undernutrition, contradicting the prevailing causal assumption.
